body.label: source.root.thm
body.claim: 2 is an upper bound for the set of real numbers whose square is less than 4.

body.label: source.root.goal
body.claim: 2 is an upper bound of S, i.e. ∀ x ∈ S, x ≤ 2.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For every real number x, x² = x * x.
deduced_from_theorem.name: Definition of square
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to rewrite x² as x * x.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For every real number x, 0 ≤ x * x.
deduced_from_theorem.name: Nonnegativity of squares
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied to the arbitrary real number x.

proof_steps.proof_method: By the order-field axiom that squares are nonnegative.
Rewrite x * x as x² using x² = x * x.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If an assertion P(x) holds for an arbitrary element x of a type, then ∀ x, P(x).
deduced_from_theorem.name: Universal introduction
deduced_from_theorem.description: Used to conclude the universally quantified theorem.

proof_steps.proof_method: Since x : ℝ was arbitrary, generalize the result.

proof.text:
Proof of Lemma 1.
  For each x : ℝ, x² = x * x.  By the order‐field axiom that squares are nonnegative, 0 ≤ x * x. □

body.label: source.root.lemma2
body.claim: ∀ a b : ℝ, 0 ≤ a → 0 ≤ b → a < b → sqrt a < sqrt b.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For real numbers x and y, under the required nonnegativity hypotheses, sqrt x < sqrt y is equivalent to x < y.
deduced_from_theorem.name: real.sqrt_lt_iff_lt
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied with x = a and y = b.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For every real number x, 0 ≤ sqrt x.
deduced_from_theorem.description: Supplies the needed nonnegativity facts for square roots.

proof_steps.claim: ∀ a b : ℝ, 0 ≤ a → 0 ≤ b → a < b → sqrt a < sqrt b.
proof_steps.proof_method: This follows directly from `real.sqrt_lt_iff_lt` together with the fact that `sqrt` maps nonnegative real numbers to nonnegative real numbers.

proof.text:
Proof of Lemma 2.
  This follows from the property `real.sqrt_lt_iff_lt` and the fact that `sqrt` maps nonnegatives to nonnegatives. □

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For every real number x, 0 ≤ x².
deduced_from_theorem.description: Ensures the argument x² is nonnegative so the defining property of real.sqrt applies.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For every real number x, |x| = sqrt (x²).
deduced_from_theorem.name: abs_eq_sqrt_sq
deduced_from_theorem.description: Identifies the absolute value with the square root of the square.

proof_steps.proof_method: By the defining property of real.sqrt on nonnegative arguments together with abs_eq_sqrt_sq.
Since x was arbitrary, generalize the equality to all real x.

proof.text:
Proof of Lemma 3.
  This is the defining property of `real.sqrt` on nonnegative arguments together with `abs_eq_sqrt_sq`. □

body.label: source.root.lemma4
body.claim: ∀ a b : ℝ, a < b → a ≤ b.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For all real numbers a and b, if a < b, then a ≤ b.
deduced_from_theorem.name: lt.le
deduced_from_theorem.description: This axiom exactly matches the goal.

proof_steps.claim: ∀ a b : ℝ, a < b → a ≤ b.

proof.text:
Proof of Lemma 4.
  This is the axiom `lt.le`. □

deduced_from_theorem.claim: For every real number x, -|x| ≤ x and x ≤ |x|.
deduced_from_theorem.name: basic property of absolute value
deduced_from_theorem.description: Obtained by unfolding the definition of absolute value.

proof_steps.proof_method: This is the definition of absolute value.

proof.text:
Proof of Lemma 5.
  This is the definition of `abs`. □

deduced_from_theorem.name: Lemma 5
deduced_from_theorem.description: Specialized at x and projected to the second conjunct.

proof_steps.proof_method: from Lemma 5, specialize at x and take the conjunct x ≤ |x|
proof_steps.statement: From Lemma 5 we have h₅.1 : x ≤ |x|.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: If a ≤ b and b < c, then a < c.
deduced_from_theorem.name: transitivity of ≤ and <
deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied with a = x, b = |x|, and c = 2.

proof_steps.proof_method: from h₅.1 and h₁₀ by transitivity of ≤ and <
proof_steps.statement: From h₅.1 and h₁₀, by transitivity of ≤ and <, we get h₆₁ : x < 2.

deduced_from_theorem.claim: ∀ a b : ℝ, a < b → a ≤ b.
deduced_from_theorem.name: Lemma 4
deduced_from_theorem.description: Specialized to a = x and b = 2.

proof_steps.statement: From h₆₁ and Lemma 4 we conclude x ≤ 2.

proof.text:
Proof of Lemma 6.
  Let x : ℝ and h₁₀ : |x| < 2.
  From Lemma 5 we have h₅.1 : x ≤ |x|.
  From h₅.1 and h₁₀, by transitivity of ≤ and <, we get h₆₁ : x < 2.
  From h₆₁ and Lemma 4 we conclude x ≤ 2. □

body.text:
Main proof

Let x : ℝ and assume h : x² < 4.
1. From Lemma 1 we have h₁ : 0 ≤ x².
2. From h₁, the fact 0 ≤ 4, and h, Lemma 2 gives
     sqrt (x²) < sqrt 4 = 2.
3. Lemma 3 yields |x| = sqrt (x²), so |x| < 2.
4. Lemma 6 applied to |x| < 2 yields x ≤ 2.

Since x : ℝ with x² < 4 was arbitrary, we conclude ∀ x ∈ S, x ≤ 2. Hence 2 is an upper bound of S.
